name: Caniça Trail (PR10 Seia)
name_original: Rota da Caniça (PR 10)  — Serra da Estrela
description: Hike the moderate 4.5-mile Tour of Caniça (PR 10) in Serra da Estrela, featuring waterfalls, ancient forests, and unique rock formations.
region_intro: Hiking around Lapa Dos Dinheiros offers access to varied terrain within the Serra da Estrela region of Portugal. The landscape features include forested areas, river valleys, and elevated viewpoints, with several lakes and dams contributing to the natural scenery. The region provides a network of paths suitable for those seeking easy hiking trails. Best easy hiking trails around Lapa Dos Dinheiros The most popular easy hiking route is Tour da Eira (PR 4) — circular route in Serra da Estrela, a 2.0 miles (3.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the surrounding Serra da Estrela landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake loop from Serra da Estrela, an easy 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This loop trail explores the area around Covão do Curral Dam and Covão do Ferro Lake. Local hikers also love the Long Lake loop from Albufeira do Covão do Forno, a 2.3 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through a landscape featuring a long lake, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Hiking around Lapa Dos Dinheiros is defined by forested valleys, mountain views, and serene lake environments. The network offers options for various ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails suitable for beginners and families. The routes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Lapa Dos Dinheiros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: There are 14 easy hiking trails around Lapa Dos Dinheiros, offering a variety of landscapes from forested areas to river valleys and elevated viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy circular hiking routes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2174795785/tour-da-eira-pr-4-circular-route-in-serra-da-estrela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour da Eira (PR 4)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 2.0-mile (3.3 km) loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10370014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake loop&lt;/a&gt; is another easy 3.2-mile (5.1 km) option.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: The best time for easy hikes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be warm, and winters may bring snow to higher elevations of Serra da Estrela, making some trails more challenging.

Q: Are the easy trails around Lapa Dos Dinheiros suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the easy trails are generally suitable for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17313258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake loop from Albufeira do Covão do Forno&lt;/a&gt;, at 2.3 miles (3.6 km), offer gentle terrain and scenic views that are enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the easy hiking trails in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: Most trails in the Serra da Estrela region, including those around Lapa Dos Dinheiros, are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to carry waste bags.

Q: Are there any waterfalls on the easy hiking routes?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls on some easy routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17313329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caniçada Waterfall loop from Lapa dos Dinheiros&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.3-mile (3.7 km) trail that takes you to the beautiful Caniçada Waterfall. Another notable waterfall nearby is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5904227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço da Broca de Serapitel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on easy hikes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: Easy hikes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros offer diverse views, including forested valleys, serene lake environments, and panoramic mountain vistas of the Serra da Estrela. Trails often lead to viewpoints overlooking the landscape, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Serra da Estrela loop from Sabugueiro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see near the easy trails?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the trails, you can explore natural landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/974890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lapa dos Dinheiros River Beach&lt;/a&gt; is also a pleasant spot to visit.

Q: What should I bring for an easy hike in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: Even for easy hikes, it's advisable to bring comfortable walking shoes, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light jacket as mountain weather can change quickly. A basic first-aid kit is also recommended.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads for easy hikes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in or near the villages and starting points of many trails around Lapa Dos Dinheiros. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages or local signage.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: The easy trails in Lapa Dos Dinheiros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained paths, the peaceful natural surroundings, and the accessibility for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there places to eat or have a coffee after an easy hike?
A: Yes, Lapa Dos Dinheiros and nearby villages offer local cafes and restaurants where you can enjoy a meal or a coffee after your hike. These establishments often provide traditional regional cuisine.
tips:
Nestled on the banks of the Caniça river, a tributary of the Alva river, this beach area is a haven in the middle of a mountainous landscape, where ancient chestnut trees and granite blocks shape the scenery.

The crystal-clear, inviting water is perfect for a refreshing dip. The beach, which has been awarded the title of accessible, has lifeguards and a bar, making the experience even more enjoyable.
100m detour from the hiking trail beautiful view of the waterfalls

This hike is really beautiful. This is the regional hiking trail PR10, which is marked with yellow-red markings. It is advisable to follow these signs, we did not do that at the beginning because we only discovered during the course of the tour that this Komoot tour is the official PR10. So, especially at the beginning, follow the markings rather than the app. But it definitely needs good step resistance, in between you have to climb quite a bit, which we liked very much. Really recommendable!

The enigmatic Devil's Horns rise from the granite, defying the sky with their six-metre height. Sculpted by erosion over the centuries, they earned this bold name thanks to their sharp and imposing profile.

But the charm doesn't end there: around this unique formation, there are small crystal-clear lagoons and serene waterfalls, creating a true natural refuge that seems to have come straight out of a fantasy story.

faqs.question: Where does the Tour of Caniça (PR 10) trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in Lapa dos Dinheiros. You can find parking available in the village, often near the Lapa dos Dinheiros River Beach area, which serves as a common trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Tour of Caniça (PR 10) trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While it's only about 7 km long, it involves an accumulated elevation gain of nearly 500 meters, with some steep climbs, particularly after the Cornos do Diabo and on the ascent to "Buraco do Sumo." It requires good physical fitness and is better suited for those with some hiking experience.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Caniça (PR 10) suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, including steep and technical sections with rock walls and areas requiring some scrambling, the trail is not recommended for young children or individuals with balance issues. It's best for hikers with some experience and good physical fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse terrain, including woodlands, pine forests, meadows, and rocky outcrops. A significant portion goes through the Souto da Lapa, an ancient chestnut forest. You'll also encounter dirt roads, a huge levada (irrigation channel), and sections where the stream runs underground.

faqs.question: What are the main natural attractions and landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in natural beauty. Key attractions include the charming Souto da Lapa chestnut forest, the inviting Lapa dos Dinheiros River Beach, the impressive Caniçada Waterfall, the intriguing Cornos do Diabo rock formation, and the mysterious Buraco do Sumo where the stream disappears underground. You'll also pass the Aqueduct of the Caniça Route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Tour of Caniça (PR 10)?
faqs.answer: This trail is suitable for hiking year-round, except during heavy rain. It's an excellent option in winter when higher altitudes of Serra da Estrela are covered in snow, as it's at a lower elevation and generally snow-free. The Caniçada Waterfall is particularly powerful during the winter rainy season.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Caniça (PR 10) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, it's important to note that some sections are steep and technical. If bringing a dog, ensure they are agile and accustomed to challenging terrain. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near steep drops, and be mindful of other hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Serra da Estrela Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Tour of Caniça (PR 10) or other trails within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: Does the Tour of Caniça (PR 10) intersect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route primarily follows the Rota da Caniça. It also briefly intersects with sections of the Grande Rota das Aldeias Históricas and the Trilho T2 (PNSE) - Lapa dos Dinheiros / Valezim.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the Tour of Caniça (PR 10) trail?
faqs.answer: Water is a constant feature on this trail, with the Ribeira da Caniça stream running alongside much of the path and a huge levada carrying water. However, it's always recommended to carry your own sufficient supply of drinking water, as natural sources may not be potable.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for hiking the Tour of Caniça (PR 10)?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for the most challenging climbs in the middle of the route, particularly after passing the Cornos do Diabo. While the trail is well-marked, some areas might have less clear signage, so downloading the GPS trail beforehand is highly advisable to stay on track.
